reforms.description: A major reform has been proposed to overhaul the personal income tax system, which includes a substantial personal tax credit and adjustments to tax brackets aimed at improving work incentives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to reduce effective marginal tax rates, thereby enhancing incentives to work and potentially increasing labor force participation.
reforms.status_evidence: A major reform has just been proposed.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposed overhaul of the personal income tax system represents a significant structural change aimed at improving economic incentives and labor market outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: A major reform has just been proposed to the personal income tax system.

reforms.description: Iceland is advised to increase educational inputs by raising the number of school days and enhancing resources for vocational programmes and universities to improve human capital development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving educational inputs is expected to enhance the skills and capabilities of the workforce, leading to higher productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been advised but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change aimed at enhancing human capital development.
reforms.source_quote: Iceland would be well advised to boost its educational inputs: by increasing the number of school-days at least as rapidly as currently planned.

reforms.description: Iceland's government increased funding to the Technological Fund by 20% in 1995 to support research and technology initiatives, aiming to enhance the country's R&D capacity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for research and technology initiatives is expected to bolster R&D capacity and drive innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding increase was executed in 1995.
reforms.importance_rationale: The funding increase is significant but does not represent a complete overhaul of the funding system.
reforms.source_quote: Efforts to boost R&D spending include a 20 per cent increase in funding to the Technological Fund in 1995.

reforms.description: The establishment of the Icelandic Research Council in 1994 aimed to encourage local firms to engage in European technology programmes, enhancing access to foreign technology and know-how.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging local firms to engage in European technology programmes is expected to improve innovation capabilities and access to advanced technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The council was established in 1994 and has been operational since.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the council is significant for enhancing innovation governance but does not represent a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The Icelandic Research Council was established in 1994 to encourage Icelandic firms to participate in European technology programmes.
